The first molecule has a low SA score (~8.93) and presumably a lower BRD4, while the second molecule has better BRD4 (~10.76) but worse SA. 

A better molecule would combine the favorable fragments that might improve binding and drug-likeness while simplifying the structure to reduce SA. The first molecule is simpler with a fluorophenyl and benzamide core. The second molecule has a more complex heterocyclic motif linked via an alkene linker.

Proposal: Use the core amide and methoxy-substituted phenyl ring from the first molecule for drug-likeness and synthetic accessibility, but introduce a simplified heterocycle from the second molecule (for improved binding) without the complicated side chains to keep SA low. Specifically, replace the benzamide portion in molecule 1 with a fused pyrimidine-pyrazole ring as in molecule 2, but remove the isopropyl and methyl substitutions to reduce complexity. This should keep high binding affinity while improving synthetic accessibility.

Proposed molecule:

COc1ccc(F)cc1NC(=O)c2nc3cnn(C)c3c(C)c2

This is a methoxy-fluorophenyl connected via amide to a simplified methylated fused pyrimidine-pyrazole ring. It keeps the beneficial pharmacophore while reducing side chain complexity.
